ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Aam Panna exists; closest defensible match is 'mango beverage' (FOODON_03311737). Aam Panna is a spiced raw-mango drink with black salt and cumin.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Aam Panna. A00LC (fruit-based beverages) is the closest defensible category anchor. Spiced raw-mango preparation is not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Aam Panna or raw mango beverage as of 2026.

preparation_method: boiling or roasting raw green mangoes
seasoning with roasted cumin, black salt, and mint

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Aam Panna is one glass of approximately 250 ml, providing around 120 kcal, 30 g of carbohydrates, and meaningful amounts of Vitamin C and electrolytes including potassium and sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Aam Panna is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN tables. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN raw green mango composition data combined with USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) entries for raw mango (FDC ID 169910) and adjusted for dilution with water, added sugar (~4 g/100 ml), black salt, and cumin. Sodium is elevated relative to plain mango juice due to black salt addition. All values represent a typical home-prepared Aam Panna at approximately 1:3 mango pulp-to-water ratio with moderate sugar.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and analogous estimate from raw mango GI data
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Aam Panna exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 45 is estimated by analogy to raw green mango juice (GI ~41–50, low category) as reported in peer-reviewed GI tables. The acidity of raw mango and presence of dietary fibre lower the glycemic response relative to ripe mango juice (GI ~60). Added sugar (sucrose, GI ~65) moderately raises the composite GI. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving = (45 × 30 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 13.5 g, classified as medium GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium from black salt (kala namak); offset by potassium content
hypertension.guidance: Aam Panna contains approximately 120 mg sodium per 100 ml (300 mg per 250 ml serving) primarily from black salt; individuals on a strict low-sodium diet (<1500 mg/day) should limit portion size or prepare the drink with minimal or no black salt while retaining the potassium-rich mango base, which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar raises postprandial glucose; low GI of raw mango base is partially offset by sucrose addition
type2_diabetes.guidance: The raw mango base has a low glycemic index (~45) and provides fibre, but a standard 250 ml serving delivers approximately 30 g of carbohydrates including 26 g of sugar when prepared with conventional sugar; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute sugar with stevia or a low-calorie sweetener, limit serving to 150 ml, and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat and cholesterol; potassium and Vitamin C support vascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Aam Panna is extremely low in fat (0.1 g/100 ml) and cholesterol-free, and provides potassium (110 mg/100 ml) and Vitamin C (18 mg/100 ml), both of which support endothelial function; it is generally suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when prepared with reduced sugar and moderate black salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vitamin C and cumin provide anti-inflammatory potential
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high Vitamin C content and cumin in Aam Panna offer anti-inflammatory benefits; however, individuals whose arthritis symptoms are aggravated by acidic foods should consume cautiously and monitor personal tolerance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aam Panna is a traditional Indian summer beverage prepared from raw (unripe) green mangoes, widely consumed across North India and the broader Indian subcontinent as a cooling drink during the hot summer months. The preparation involves boiling or roasting whole raw mangoes until the pulp softens, then extracting the tart pulp and blending it with chilled water at approximately a 1:3 ratio. The drink is sweetened with sugar or jaggery and seasoned with roasted cumin powder (bhuna jeera), black salt (kala namak), and fresh mint leaves, producing a distinctive balance of sour, sweet, and savory flavors with a refreshing, slightly tangy taste and a thin, juice-like consistency. Nutritionally, Aam Panna provides approximately 48 kcal per 100 ml, 12 g of carbohydrates, and meaningful amounts of Vitamin C (18 mg), Vitamin A (45 mcg RAE), potassium (110 mg), and iron (0.8 mg). The sodium content is elevated (~120 mg/100 ml) due to black salt. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low category) based on the acidity and fibre of raw mango, though added sugar moderately raises the glycemic load to approximately 13.5 per 250 ml serving. Clinically, Aam Panna is valued for its electrolyte-replenishing properties and its traditional use in preventing heat stroke (Loo). It is suitable for most adults but should be consumed with reduced sugar by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and with reduced black salt by those with hypertension or on sodium-restricted diets. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Is Aam Panna good for diabetes?

confidence_reason: Aam Panna is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN raw green mango composition and USDA SR Legacy raw mango data (FDC ID 169910), adjusted for typical home-preparation dilution, sugar addition, and black salt seasoning. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed raw mango juice GI data. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source provides a direct Aam Panna entry.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent a typical home-prepared Aam Panna at approximately 1:3 mango pulp-to-water ratio with ~4 g sugar per 100 ml and black salt. Recipes vary significantly and sodium content in particular may range from 50–250 mg/100 ml depending on black salt quantity. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central raw mango entry). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no UV-exposed or animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — entirely plant-based preparation). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no hydrogenated fats in any ingredient). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.03), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.02), and saturated_fat_g (0.02) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy raw mango fatty acid profile scaled to the diluted beverage; these values are trace-level. 4. water_g (86.5) is estimated from the dilution ratio and raw mango moisture content (~83 g/100 g per USDA). 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Aam Panna was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. 6. Portions in the input JSON contained four identical duplicate entries; deduplicated to one standard serving of 250 ml. 7. The input food_group was listed as 'Snacks'; reclassified to 'Beverages' as Aam Panna is a liquid drink. 8. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for Aam Panna as of 2026.
